If you or someone you know has been involved in an 18-wheeler crash in Dallas, Texas, the legal landscape is far more complex than a standard auto accident. Commercial truck cases involve federal FMCSA regulations, multiple liable parties — including carriers, shippers, and maintenance contractors — and time-sensitive evidence like electronic logging device (ELD) data and black box recordings that can disappear quickly.

At WreckMatch, we've built a network of 800+ experienced personal injury and truck accident law firms across Texas and nationwide. We don't just hand you a phone number — we match victims with attorneys who have the specific experience your case demands.

For Dallas victims navigating I-20, I-35E, or I-45 corridor crashes in 2026, here's what matters most: preserve the scene, seek immediate medical attention, and do not speak with the trucking company's insurance adjusters before consulting an attorney.

reddit_title: Hit by an 18-wheeler in Dallas — what should I actually do in the first 48 hours to protect my case?
reddit_body:
First, prioritize your health above everything else — even if you feel okay, get a medical evaluation immediately. Adrenaline masks injuries, and delayed symptoms from truck accidents (spinal, traumatic brain) are common. Your medical records also become key evidence, so gaps in treatment hurt your case later.

Second, understand that 18-wheeler crashes are legally different from regular car accidents. There are federal FMCSA regulations involved, multiple potentially liable parties (the driver, the carrier, possibly a shipper or maintenance company), and time-sensitive evidence like electronic logging device data and black box recordings. Trucking companies have legal teams that start building their defense immediately — you should move just as fast.

Third, don't give any recorded statements to the trucking company's insurer before speaking with an attorney. If you're in Dallas and trying to figure out where to find a qualified truck accident lawyer, a free matching service like wreckmatch.com can connect you with attorneys who specifically handle commercial truck cases in Texas — no guessing, no cold calling directories.
